heading: Sample Data
body:
This quick start uses the `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection which
contains details about movies. In each document in the collection, the
`plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field contains a vector embedding that represents the string
in the `plot` field. For more information on the schema of the documents in
the collection, see [sample data](mflix-embedded_movies).

By storing your source data and its corresponding vector embeddings in the
same document, you can leverage both fields for complex queries or
[hybrid search](avs-reciprocal-rank-fusion). You can even store vector
embeddings generated from different [embedding model](avs-key-concepts)
in the same document to streamline your workflow as you test the performance
of different vector embedding models for your specific use case.

This quick start uses the `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection which
contains details about movies. In each document in the collection, the
`plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field contains a vector embedding that represents the string
in the `plot` field. For more information on the schema of the documents in
the collection, see [sample data](mflix-embedded_movies).

By storing your source data and its corresponding vector embeddings in the
same document, you can leverage both fields for complex queries or
[hybrid search](avs-reciprocal-rank-fusion). You can even store vector
embeddings generated from different [embedding model](avs-key-concepts)
in the same document to streamline your workflow as you test the performance
of different vector embedding models for your specific use case.

This quick start uses the `sample_mflix` database, which contains collections
with movie details.

The `fullplot` field in the `movies` collection contains text that
describes the plot of the movie.

By configuring MongoDB Vector Search to automatically generate embeddings for text data in
your collection, such as the `fullplot` field in the `movies` collection,
you can use MongoDB Vector Search to also generate embeddings for natural language
queries against the `fullplot` field. MongoDB Vector Search can use the Voyage AI API (Application Programming Interface)
keys to generate embeddings from available Voyage AI embedding models.

This quick start uses the `sample_mflix` database, which contains collections
that contains detail about movies.

The `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field in the `embedded_movies`
collection contain the a vector embedding that represents the string
in the `plot` field. For more information on the schema of the documents in
the collection, see [sample data](mflix-embedded_movies).

By storing your source data and its corresponding vector embeddings in the
same document, you can leverage both fields for complex queries or
[hybrid search](avs-reciprocal-rank-fusion). You can even store vector
embeddings generated from different [embedding model](avs-key-concepts)
in the same document to streamline your workflow as you test the performance
of different vector embedding models for your specific use case.
